name: Barghest
description: The Barghest is a mythical monstrous black dog from Northern English folklore, particularly associated with Yorkshire and Lincolnshire. It is often described as a large, spectral black dog with glowing red eyes and sharp teeth. Its appearance is considered an omen of death, and its howls are said to foretell impending doom or tragedy. The Barghest is a shapeshifter, sometimes appearing as a headless man, a white cat, or even a rabbit. It is believed to guard gates, crossroads, and lonely roads, preying on travelers or warning them of danger. Tales of the Barghest are used to frighten children and keep them from wandering alone at night. Some believe it is a malevolent spirit, while others see it as a guardian of the land, only appearing to those who are in danger or about to die. Its presence is often associated with graveyards, dark lanes, and other liminal spaces, marking the boundary between the living and the dead. The Barghest embodies the fears of the unknown and the dangers lurking in the dark corners of the countryside, reflecting a time when the natural world was perceived as both powerful and unpredictable.
subtitle: Omen of death, spectral black dog
appearance: A large, spectral black dog with glowing red eyes. It is often described as having shaggy fur, sharp teeth, and a powerful build. Its size varies in different accounts, but it is generally depicted as larger than an average dog, sometimes even the size of a calf. Its presence is often accompanied by a chilling wind or a sulfurous smell.
habitat: Lonely roads, graveyards, crossroads, dark lanes, and moors in Northern England, particularly Yorkshire and Lincolnshire.
behavior: The Barghest is a solitary creature that is often seen as a harbinger of death. It is said to roam the countryside at night, howling and terrifying those who cross its path. Some accounts portray it as a malevolent spirit that actively seeks to harm humans, while others depict it as a guardian of the land that only appears to warn people of danger. Its behavior is unpredictable and often influenced by the emotions and fears of those who encounter it.

keyMyths.title: The Barghest of Troller's Gill
keyMyths.description: A tale from Yorkshire tells of a Barghest that haunted a deep ravine, terrorizing the local villagers and causing livestock to disappear. It was eventually driven away by a brave farmer who confronted it with a burning torch and a crucifix.
